category_title: At-risk four-year-old students exhibiting varied oral language behaviors
minimal_context: The teacher is leading a storybook reading activity during circle time to help the student develop language skills.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are reading the storybook now. What is the animal on this page?

STUDENT: Dog.

TUTOR: That is correct. What is the dog doing in the picture?

STUDENT: Run.

TUTOR: No, that is incorrect. He is jumping. Where is he jumping?

STUDENT: (Looks down, hesitant to use more words) Bed.

options.A: Observe the student covertly during activities without engaging, intervening minimally and only for disputes.
options.B: Model complete sentences as often as possible during teacher-directed activities like circle time.
options.C: Assign the student a seat near peers who can serve as models during circle time.
options.D: Avoid using teacher-selected storybooks and instead allow the student to self-select books of interest.
